equipment: Manages equipment and asset inventory tracking. Handles CRUD operations for equipment items with fields like equipment_id (CRL####), name, manufacturer, model, serial_number, status (Available/Allocated/In Use/Faulty/Returned/Quarantined), area_id (storage location), job_id (assignment), verification_type (CALIBRATED/INSPECTED/PAT TESTED), expires_on, generic_name (category like DRILL, STOP BOARD, JIGSAW). Use for listing, adding, updating, or querying equipment items.
job: Manages jobs, projects, and work orders. Handles jobs with fields like job_id (NNN-NNNN format), job_name, client_name, status (Pending/Active/On Hold/Completed/Cancelled), site, requested_by, date_from, date_to, equipment_requests (JSON array), materials (JSON array with id, name, quantity). Jobs are the primary organizational unit for tracking work and assigning resources.
area: Manages storage areas and locations within the inventory system. Handles areas with fields like area_id, name, location_name, location_type (Warehouse/Van/Site/Storage). Areas are used to track where equipment and materials are stored.
material: Manages consumable materials and supplies inventory. Handles materials with fields like material_id, name, quantity, unit, reorder_level, job_id (assignment), area_id (storage location). Use for tracking consumables, supplies, and materials that get depleted.
import: Imports data from CSV or Excel files. Processes bulk data to import equipment, materials, and areas with automatic field mapping, validation, and ID generation. Accepts filePath, entityType (equipment/material/area), and optional dryRun mode.
wipe-data: Administrative function to clear database data. Use only when user explicitly requests to wipe, clear, or reset data. Requires confirmation before execution. Dangerous operation.
show-help: Displays help information about available commands and system capabilities. Use when user asks for help, guidance, or wants to know what they can do.
